title: The window cleaner
summary: A man cleans the windows of a Vienna café every Tuesday morning. He says he does not watch — he notices.
body:
Mr Anton cleans the windows of a small café in Vienna. He works on Tuesday mornings. The café opens at seven. Mr Anton comes at six. He has done this for nineteen years.

The café has three big windows. They look out at the square. Mr Anton brings a small ladder, a soft cloth, and a bottle of water with vinegar. He does not like sprays. He says the sprays leave a smell that customers can taste in their coffee.

While Mr Anton works, he sees many things. He sees the same old man read the same newspaper at the table by the window. He sees the same young woman who orders the same hot chocolate every Tuesday. He sees a small dog tied to a bench. The dog is not always the same dog, but the bench is.

The owner of the café once asked Mr Anton, 'Do you watch us?'

Mr Anton smiled. He said, 'I do not watch. I notice. It is different. A watcher wants to see something new. A noticer wants to see if something old is still there.'

The owner did not understand at first. Then she did. She gave Mr Anton a cup of coffee.

He drinks it on the small ladder. He drinks slowly. Then he cleans the last window.

annotations.meaningVi: người quan sát / người nhìn ngó

grammarNotes.title: Present simple with 'the same' for habits
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi nói về việc lặp lại với cùng một đối tượng, A1 dùng 'the same + danh từ' kết hợp hiện tại đơn. Trong bài: 'He sees the same old man read the same newspaper.' 'She orders the same hot chocolate every Tuesday.' Đối lập với 'a + danh từ' (một, bất kỳ): 'a small dog tied to a bench.' Cấu trúc này nhấn mạnh thói quen và sự không đổi.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'the same + noun' with the present simple to describe repeated habits with the same object. 'He sees the same old man read the same newspaper.' 'She orders the same hot chocolate every Tuesday.' Compare with 'a + noun' (any one): 'a small dog tied to a bench.' This structure emphasises constancy in a routine.

translationVi:
Ông Anton lau cửa sổ của một quán cà phê nhỏ ở Vienna. Ông làm việc vào các sáng thứ Ba. Quán mở lúc bảy giờ. Ông Anton đến lúc sáu giờ. Ông làm việc này mười chín năm rồi.

Quán có ba cửa sổ to. Cửa sổ nhìn ra quảng trường. Ông Anton mang một cái thang nhỏ, một miếng vải mềm, và một chai nước pha giấm. Ông không thích bình xịt. Ông nói bình xịt để lại mùi mà khách có thể nếm thấy trong tách cà phê.

Trong lúc làm, ông Anton thấy nhiều thứ. Ông thấy cùng một cụ ông đọc cùng một tờ báo ở cái bàn cạnh cửa sổ. Ông thấy cùng một cô gái trẻ gọi cùng một ly sô-cô-la nóng vào mỗi thứ Ba. Ông thấy một con chó nhỏ buộc vào ghế. Con chó không phải lúc nào cũng cùng con, nhưng cái ghế thì đúng.

Chủ quán một lần hỏi ông Anton: 'Ông có để mắt nhìn chúng tôi không?'

Ông Anton mỉm cười. Ông nói: 'Tôi không nhìn. Tôi để ý. Khác đó. Người nhìn muốn thấy cái gì mới. Người để ý muốn thấy cái gì cũ còn ở đó hay không.'

Lúc đầu chủ quán không hiểu. Sau đó cô hiểu. Cô đưa ông Anton một tách cà phê.

Ông uống nó ngay trên thang. Ông uống chậm. Rồi ông lau nốt cái cửa sổ cuối.

questions.explanation: 'Mr Anton cleans the windows of a small café in Vienna.'
'He works on Tuesday mornings… Mr Anton comes at six.'

questions.prompt: How long has he done this work?
questions.explanation: 'He has done this for nineteen years.'

questions.prompt: What does he bring to clean the windows?
options: A spray bottle and paper towels
A small ladder, a soft cloth, and a bottle of water with vinegar
questions.explanation: 'Mr Anton brings a small ladder, a soft cloth, and a bottle of water with vinegar.'

questions.prompt: Why does he not like sprays?
options: They leave a smell that customers can taste in their coffee.
His mother told him not to.
questions.explanation: 'He says the sprays leave a smell that customers can taste in their coffee.'

questions.prompt: What does the same old man do every week?
options: Reads the same newspaper at the window table
questions.explanation: 'He sees the same old man read the same newspaper at the table by the window.'

questions.prompt: What is always the same about the dog scene?
options: The bench — the dog is not always the same
questions.explanation: 'The dog is not always the same dog, but the bench is.'

questions.prompt: What question does the café owner ask?
What is the difference between 'watching' and 'noticing', according to Mr Anton?
options: A watcher wants to see something new; a noticer wants to see if something old is still there.
Watching is fast; noticing is slow.
Watching is in summer; noticing is in winter.
Watching is rude; noticing is illegal.
questions.hint: Mr Anton's reply to the owner.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Quiet attention to the same things, week after week, is a small kind of care.
Old men should not read newspapers.
questions.explanation: Mr Anton's careful, repeated attention is a form of unspoken care.
questions.hint: Think about what Mr Anton's small habit gives the café.

blanks.explanation: The windows look out at the square.
A small dog tied to a bench.
The owner asks 'Do you watch us?'
She gave him a cup of coffee.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Anton says a watcher wants to see something new, but a noticer wants to see if something old is still there. Is there a place in your life where you have been a 'noticer' for years without realising it? What small same-thing have you been quietly checking on, week after week?
